companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: NVIDIA's Palantir partnership produced a Sovereign AI Operating System (announced June 29) that drove Palantir stock up 9% today. Anthropic's Claude models in Microsoft Foundry on GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs are now generally available. NVIDIA runs 81% of TOP500 supercomputers with 90% of new entries; FY2026 revenue: $215.9B (+65% YoY). At ISC High Performance 2026 in Hamburg, NVIDIA launched the BioNeMo Agent Toolkit and disclosed 35 European Vera Rubin HPC systems in development.

items.headline: Palantir-NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System launched; PLTR surges 9%
items.detail: Palantir and NVIDIA announced the Sovereign AI Operating System on June 29 — a combined hardware and software platform enabling governments and large organizations to run NVIDIA Nemotron open models in air-gapped Blackwell Ultra environments. Agencies own the model weights and training data on their own infrastructure. The announcement drove Palantir stock up 9% to $127.31 on July 1.

items.headline: Claude models in Microsoft Foundry on GB300 Blackwell Ultra now generally available
items.detail: Anthropic's Claude models are now generally available in Microsoft Foundry, hosted on Microsoft Azure and running on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs. This completes the deployment channel connecting Anthropic's models to enterprise Azure customers through NVIDIA hardware.

items.headline: NVIDIA runs 81% of TOP500; BioNeMo Agent Toolkit live; 35 European Vera Rubin systems in development
items.detail: NVIDIA technology runs 81% of the TOP500 supercomputers with 90% of new entries. At ISC High Performance 2026 in Hamburg, NVIDIA launched the BioNeMo Agent Toolkit enabling AI agents to gather evidence, reason across findings, run computational experiments, and accelerate scientific discovery. A record 35 AI HPC systems using Vera Rubin are in development across European research institutions. FY2026 revenue: $215.9B (+65% YoY), operating income $130.4B, diluted EPS $4.90.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains delayed to July with no confirmed release date as of July 1. The Gemini Managed Agents API entered public preview with the Antigravity Agent. Nobel laureate John Jumper — who departed DeepMind for Anthropic in late June — made his first public appearance today at Anthropic's Claude Science AI for Science event. Alphabet's $84.75B equity raise closed last week as the largest in corporate history.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro still delayed — no confirmed release date as of July 1
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro missed its June 30 self-imposed deadline and remains delayed with no official confirmed date as of July 1. Google is reviewing early enterprise tester feedback and refining coding, token consumption, and long-task performance before general release. The delay continues to add risk to Apple's Gemini-powered Siri AI timeline ahead of September 2026 GA. No official Google statement has confirmed a revised date.

items.headline: Gemini Managed Agents API enters public preview with Antigravity Agent
items.detail: Google launched Managed Agents in the Gemini API in public preview, enabling developers to build and deploy stateful autonomous agents running in secure, isolated Google-hosted Linux sandbox environments. The Antigravity Agent — Google's first managed agent — can autonomously plan, reason, write and execute code, manage files, and browse the web inside its sandbox. Gemini 3.5 Flash remains available for agents and coding.

items.headline: John Jumper's first public appearance since leaving DeepMind — at Anthropic's AI for Science event
items.detail: Nobel Prize winner John Jumper (AlphaFold2 co-creator) made his first public appearance since joining Anthropic from DeepMind, appearing at Anthropic's 'The Briefing: AI for Science' virtual event on June 30. Jumper was one of five senior DeepMind researchers to depart in a single week, a talent exodus that also included Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer and researchers Jonas Adler, Alexander Pritzel, and Arthur Conmy.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Microsoft 365 commercial pricing increases took effect today (July 1) alongside Copilot Cowork billing activation — tenants without configured usage-based billing lose Cowork access. Anthropic's Claude models are now generally available in Microsoft Foundry on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs. AWS Summit DC (day 2) concluded today with the Amazon $1B Forward Deployed Engineering program framed as competition to Microsoft's enterprise AI deployment services.

items.headline: M365 pricing increases and Copilot Cowork billing both take effect today (July 1)
items.detail: Microsoft 365 Business Standard with Copilot ($23.50/user/month) and Microsoft 365 Business Premium with Copilot ($32/user/month) became permanent commercial SKUs today. The Copilot Cowork Frontier preview grace period expired, meaning tenants without configured usage-based billing in the M365 Admin Center lost Cowork access. Work IQ APIs and Microsoft IQ are GA across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio.

items.headline: Claude models in Microsoft Foundry on GB300 Blackwell Ultra now GA
items.detail: Anthropic's Claude models in Microsoft Foundry — hosted on Microsoft Azure and running on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs — are now generally available. This milestone advances the Anthropic-Microsoft-NVIDIA tripartite strategic partnership and adds Claude to Foundry's model catalog alongside OpenAI's GPT models.

items.headline: Amazon's $1B FDE program at AWS Summit DC frames enterprise AI deployment as competition
items.detail: Amazon's $1 billion Forward Deployed Engineering program — announced June 30 at AWS Summit DC and explicitly modeled on Palantir's strategy — positions AWS directly against Microsoft's enterprise AI deployment services. The FDE program places thousands of engineers on-site with customers to co-develop AI solutions, compressing development from months to days. AWS Summit DC day 2 concluded today.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted organizations as of July 1; broader access is expected mid-July. OpenAI is planning to deploy GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month at up to 750 tokens per second for select customers. GeneBench-Pro — a research-level benchmark for AI agents in computational biology — was introduced alongside the GPT-5.6 preview.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, Luna still restricted to ~20 gov-vetted orgs; broader access expected mid-July
items.detail: GPT-5.6 Sol ($5/$30 per million input/output tokens), Terra ($2.50/$15), and Luna ($1/$6) remain in limited preview restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted partner organizations under U.S. government-imposed access requirements. GPT-5.5 is fully available. Sam Altman told staff he hopes to release GPT-5.6 broadly around mid-July 2026. GPT-4.5 was retired June 26 and replaced by GPT-5.5 in existing conversations.

items.headline: OpenAI to launch GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras in July at up to 750 tokens per second
items.detail: OpenAI confirmed plans to deploy GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month, targeting up to 750 tokens per second — dramatically faster than traditional GPU inference. Initial access will be limited to select customers as capacity expands. The Cerebras CS-3 architecture provides ultra-fast decode for OpenAI workloads, complementing existing compute from NVIDIA and AMD.

items.headline: GeneBench-Pro benchmark introduced — research-level AI agent evaluation for computational biology
items.detail: OpenAI introduced GeneBench-Pro, a research-level benchmark for judging AI agents in computational biology that expands GeneBench with harder, more realistic synthetic tasks, open-sources representative questions, and reports strong model results on scientific reasoning under uncertainty. GPT-5.6 Sol achieved top reported scores including 53.5% on the Virology Capabilities Test, 60.0% on Molecular Biology, and 68.4% on Human Pathogen Capabilities.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: An enormous day for Anthropic: the U.S. Department of Commerce lifted export controls on Fable 5 and Mythos 5, making both models available globally again starting today. Anthropic simultaneously launched Claude Sonnet 5 — the most agentic Sonnet yet, with near-Opus 4.8 performance at roughly 60% lower cost — as the new default for Free and Pro plans. Claude Science grants are open through July 15. Series H: $65B at $965B post-money; ARR: $47B; October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target.

items.headline: U.S. lifts export controls on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 — both available globally starting today
items.detail: The U.S. Department of Commerce notified Anthropic that it had removed export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5. Fable 5 is available starting today (July 1) to users globally on the Claude Platform, Claude.ai, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork. For Pro, Max, Team, and select Enterprise plans, Fable 5 is included for up to 50% of weekly usage limits through July 7, after which it moves to usage credits. The controls — imposed June 12 requiring restriction of access to foreign nationals — had forced suspension of both models for all users due to inability to verify nationality in real time.

items.headline: Claude Sonnet 5 launched — near-Opus performance, $2/$10 per Mtok intro pricing, now default for Free and Pro
items.detail: Anthropic launched Claude Sonnet 5 as its most agentic Sonnet yet, with performance close to Opus 4.8 on most tasks at roughly 60% lower cost. Sonnet 5 is available at introductory pricing of $2 per million input tokens and $10 per million output tokens through August 31, 2026, then moves to standard pricing of $3/$15. It is now the default model for Free and Pro plans. Key capabilities include multi-step planning, browser and terminal tool use, self-verification, and improved agentic search across large codebases. Prompt caching provides up to 90% cost savings; batch processing provides up to 50% savings.

items.headline: Claude Science grants open through July 15 — up to $30K for 50 selected research projects
items.detail: Following yesterday's Claude Science launch at the AI for Science virtual event, Anthropic opened grant applications for up to $30,000 in Claude credits for 50 selected AI for Science research projects. The application window closes July 15, 2026. Claude Science connects to more than 60 scientific databases and includes prebuilt toolkits for genomics, proteomics, structural biology, and cheminformatics. The workbench is in beta for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise users on macOS and Linux.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Bloomberg reported today that Meta is building a cloud business called 'Meta Compute' to sell excess AI computing capacity to outside customers — including model API access similar to AWS Bedrock and raw compute capacity similar to CoreWeave. Meta stock rose more than 10% on the report. CoreWeave (CRWV) and Nebius fell 10-14% and 12%+ respectively on competition concerns. Meta One paid subscription ($7.99 Plus / $19.99 Premium) is live across the Family of Apps.

items.headline: Bloomberg: Meta building 'Meta Compute' cloud to sell excess AI capacity — stock up 10%+
items.detail: Bloomberg reported today that Meta Platforms is developing a cloud business called Meta Compute to sell excess artificial-intelligence computing capacity to outside customers. Two offerings are planned: selling access to AI models hosted on Meta infrastructure (similar to AWS Bedrock) and selling access to raw computing capacity (similar to CoreWeave). Meta Compute is led by Santosh Janardhan (head of infrastructure), Daniel Gross (Meta Superintelligence Labs), and Dina Powell McCormick (Meta President). Meta stock rose more than 10%. A company spokesperson declined to comment.

items.headline: CoreWeave fell 10-14% to ~$87; Nebius fell 12%+ on Meta cloud competition fears
items.detail: CoreWeave (CRWV) shares fell 10-14% to approximately $87 on fears Meta could become a powerful competitor in the AI cloud market. Nebius also fell 12%+ on the report. Evercore ISI reiterated an Outperform rating and $150 price target on CoreWeave, noting the $21 billion Meta contract through December 2032 has take-or-pay protections that insulate near-term revenue. Meta cannot simply redirect spend given contracted commitments.

items.headline: Meta One paid subscription live; 2026 capex guidance $115-135B unchanged
items.detail: Meta One paid AI subscription is live across the Family of Apps: Plus at $7.99/month and Premium at $19.99/month, positioning Meta AI against ChatGPT Plus and Claude Pro. Meta's 2026 capex guidance of $115-135 billion remains unchanged. Meta Glasses (launched June 23 at $299-$399) show strong early demand with 69.2% smart glasses market share. The Meta Compute cloud plan follows SpaceX's similar move to monetize surplus compute.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: AWS Summit Washington DC concluded today (day 2, June 30-July 1). Yesterday's announcements — the $1B IC Accelerated Modernization Framework (ICAMF) for the Intelligence Community, AWS Secret Cloud for Industry (ASCI) for defense contractors, and the $1B Forward Deployed Engineering program — represent the largest single-day federal AI infrastructure commitment in AWS history. Amazon Quick desktop AI assistant is now available with Free and Plus plans.

items.headline: AWS Summit DC concludes: ICAMF $1B, ASCI, and $1B FDE program set federal AI baseline
items.detail: AWS Summit Washington DC (June 30-July 1) headlined with three major federal AI programs: ICAMF ($1B to eliminate migration costs for IC agencies through October 2030), AWS Secret Cloud for Industry (ASCI, giving defense contractors access to classified cloud infrastructure for the first time), and a $1B Forward Deployed Engineering organization placing thousands of engineers on-site with customers. CIA Director John Ratcliffe confirmed CIA will use ICAMF, saying LLMs are already helping analysts surface hidden intelligence.

items.headline: Amazon Quick desktop app now live with Free and Plus plans
items.detail: Amazon Quick — an AI assistant for work that connects to enterprise apps, learns what matters to users, and takes action on their behalf — is now available as a desktop app with Free and Plus pricing plans. Quick integrates with Amazon Connect's new agentic AI solutions for supply chain, hiring, customer experience, and healthcare. Amazon is targeting a $3 trillion valuation with AWS AI as the primary driver.

items.headline: Project Rainier live at ~500K Trainium2 chips; $100B+/10-year Anthropic agreement; $13B India investment
items.detail: Project Rainier is fully operational with nearly half a million Trainium2 chips for Anthropic Claude workloads — expected to scale to 1M+ Trainium2 chips by year end. The April 2026 expanded Anthropic agreement commits more than $100 billion over 10 years for up to 5GW of Trainium capacity. Amazon announced a $13 billion investment to expand AWS AI and cloud infrastructure in Mumbai and Hyderabad.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: Reflection AI's $150M/month Colossus 2 compute deal starts today (July 1), marking both Colossus 1 and Colossus 2 fully committed to third parties. Grok 4.5 (1.5T parameters, V9 model) remains in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX. A 2T parameter model is in the final stages of training with expected completion in late July. xAI plans a new model trained from scratch every month through the rest of 2026.

items.headline: Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts today — both Colossus facilities now fully committed
items.detail: SpaceX's $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI (valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI) begins today (July 1) at $150 million per month for NVIDIA GB300 GPU access via Colossus 2 in Memphis through 2029. Combined with Colossus 1 rented to Anthropic at approximately $1.25 billion per month, both Colossus facilities are now committed to third parties — raising questions about xAI's own future dedicated compute access for Grok.

items.headline: Grok 4.5 in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX; 2T model expected late July; monthly cadence planned
items.detail: Grok 4.5 (1.5 trillion parameters, V9 foundation model, trained on Cursor AI coding data) is running in private beta inside Tesla and SpaceX. A 2 trillion parameter model is reportedly in the final stages of training and expected to complete by late July 2026. xAI plans to release a new model trained completely from scratch every month for the remainder of 2026. Early evaluations run by SpaceX and Tesla engineers claim near-Opus performance — no independent benchmark has been published.

items.headline: SPCX ~$153 (-32% from ATH); Cursor $60B acquisition on track for Q3 close; CSAM unresolved
items.detail: SpaceX (SPCX) trades at approximately $153, down 32% from its all-time high of $225.64 on June 16. The $60 billion Cursor acquisition (Anysphere) is on track for Q3 2026 close pending regulatory approvals — Cursor has 2M+ developers and approximately $2.6 billion in annual B2B revenue. Grok adult content generation exceeds 10 billion images per month; xAI engineers acknowledged CSAM generation has no current technical fix, drawing regulatory scrutiny and $530M litigation reserve in SpaceX's IPO prospectus.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: AVGO shares are down approximately 16.5% over the past month as the AI trade cools broadly, but Broadcom's fundamental AI semiconductor trajectory remains intact. Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance: ~$16B. FY2026 full-year guidance: $56B; FY2027 target: $100B+. Jalapeño (OpenAI's first custom ASIC, co-developed with Broadcom in nine months) targets H2 2026 Stargate deployment. Broadcom has six confirmed custom-silicon customers including Google, Meta, Microsoft, Anthropic, OpenAI, and one undisclosed.

items.headline: AVGO down 16.5% past month as AI trade cools; Broadcom vs NVIDIA $100B AI race
items.detail: Broadcom shares are down approximately 16.5% over the past month alongside NVIDIA's 7.55% decline, reflecting a broader cooling of the AI trade. The two companies represent fundamentally different AI semiconductor models: NVIDIA wins through volume GPU sales across the entire market while Broadcom wins by designing custom ASICs for specific hyperscale customers. Broadcom's concentration risk means losing one mega-customer could significantly impact the math, while customer lock-in provides durable margins.

items.headline: Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance ~$16B; FY2026 $56B; FY2027 target $100B+
items.detail: Broadcom's Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue reached $10.8 billion (+143% YoY) with Q3 guidance of approximately $16 billion. Full-year FY2026 AI semiconductor guidance: $56 billion. FY2027 target: more than $100 billion. Total Q2 FY2026 revenue was a record $22.2 billion (+48% YoY). The Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform (operational since June 9) enables 20GW+ of Broadcom XPU compute through 2028.

items.headline: Jalapeño ASIC built in 9 months; six confirmed custom silicon customers; H2 2026 Stargate deployment
items.detail: Jalapeño — OpenAI's first custom AI accelerator chip, co-developed with Broadcom from design to tape-out in nine months on TSMC advanced process — targets H2 2026 deployment in Stargate data centers. Broadcom CEO Hock Tan confirmed OpenAI as Broadcom's sixth custom-silicon customer alongside Google, Meta, Microsoft, Anthropic, and one undisclosed customer. Jalapeño is a reticle-sized ASIC designed from the ground up for LLM inference with substantially better performance per watt than current alternatives.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: PLTR surged 9% to $127.31 today — a new all-time valuation high — on the NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System partnership. CEO Alex Karp appeared on CNBC calling the AI industry 'effing insane' and accusing frontier labs of overcharging and exploiting customer data. The Army NGC2 Foundry deployment is advancing. Q1 2026 revenue: $1.632B (+84.7% YoY) — the highest growth rate in company history.

items.headline: PLTR up 9% to $127.31 — new valuation high on NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS partnership
items.detail: Palantir Technologies stock surged 9% to $127.31 in midday trading today — a new all-time valuation high — after the company unveiled the Sovereign AI Operating System with NVIDIA. The system runs NVIDIA Nemotron open models on air-gapped Blackwell Ultra infrastructure for U.S. government agencies, giving customers ownership of model weights and training data on their own hardware. The stock had hit a 52-week low of $107.27 on June 25 before today's recovery.

items.headline: Alex Karp on CNBC: AI industry is 'effing insane'; criticizes frontier lab token model
items.detail: Palantir CEO Alex Karp called the AI industry 'effing insane' in a heated CNBC interview today, accusing leading AI firms of overcharging, exploiting customer data, and jeopardizing U.S. national security. Karp argued enterprises fear the token model: 'they're going to chillax and waste my time with tokens, I'm going to get no value and they're going to get my IP.' Karp's comments came the same day as the Palantir-NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS announcement, positioning Palantir as the alternative to frontier API consumption.

items.headline: Army NGC2 Foundry deployment advancing; Q1 revenue $1.632B +84.7% YoY
items.detail: The U.S. Army picked Palantir Foundry as the cloud data layer for its top modernization program, NGC2 (Next Generation Command and Control), moving from prototype to broad deployment. Major 2026 federal contract wins: $10B 10-year Army enterprise AI services, $1B 5-year DHS AI platform, $300M USDA deal (April), Treasury Department contract (June 23). Q1 2026 revenue: $1.632B (+84.7% YoY) — the highest growth rate in company history. U.S. commercial revenue: +133% to $595M. Full-year guidance raised to ~71% growth.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: iOS 27 developer beta continues with the Gemini-powered Siri AI targeting September 2026 GA. Gemini 3.5 Pro's continued delay — now into July with no confirmed date — maintains delivery risk for Apple's September iPhone launch window. OpenAI has retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice over Siri placement. Apple is evaluating Mistral as an additional model option.

items.headline: iOS 27 dev beta continues; public beta mid-July; Gemini-powered Siri AI GA September 2026
items.detail: iOS 27 developer beta — featuring a fully rebuilt Siri AI powered by Google's Gemini model — has been running since WWDC June 8. Apple's public beta is expected mid-July. Siri AI includes a dedicated standalone app, Dynamic Island integration, full on-screen awareness, and multi-step task execution through a three-tier system: on-device, Private Cloud Compute, and Gemini for complex tasks. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) remains disabled pending EU DMA regulatory clarity. GA: September 2026.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro July delay maintains risk to Apple's September Siri AI launch window
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro — expected to be the next potential Siri AI model upgrade — remains delayed with no official confirmed date. Apple's $1 billion per year Gemini deal (WWDC June 8) makes the September 2026 iPhone launch window dependent on Google delivering a refined model. DeepMind's five-researcher talent exodus in a single week compounds the delivery risk. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option to hedge provider risk.

items.headline: OpenAI weighs formal breach-of-contract notice; Apple evaluating Mistral as additional model
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over the perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement in the new Siri AI, alleging Apple made 'no honest effort' to meet integration commitments. No lawsuit has been filed as of July 1. Sherwood News reports Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option alongside Gemini, suggesting ongoing hedging across AI providers.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD Advancing AI 2026 is three weeks away (July 22-23 at Moscone Center). MI500 series specs were revealed: CDNA 6 architecture, 2nm process, HBM4E memory, targeting 1,000x AI performance improvement vs MI300X. MI450 samples are confirmed shipping to lead customers OpenAI and Meta. Oracle announced a 50,000-unit MI450 deployment for the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster in Q3 2026. MI450 demand has already exceeded AMD's own 2027 internal projections. Q2 2026 results: August 4.

items.headline: MI500 series specs revealed: CDNA 6, 2nm, HBM4E — targeting 1,000x performance vs MI300X
items.detail: AMD revealed the MI500 series roadmap targeting up to a 1,000x increase in AI performance compared to AMD Instinct MI300X GPUs introduced in 2023. The MI500 series will be built on next-generation AMD CDNA 6 architecture, advanced 2nm process technology, and cutting-edge HBM4E memory. CEO Lisa Su confirmed active customer engagement on MI500. The MI500 series targets 2027 production.

items.headline: MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI and Meta; Oracle 50K unit deployment for Q3 public supercluster
items.detail: AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to lead customers OpenAI (6GW partnership, starting with 1GW MI450 in H2 2026) and Meta ($60B/6GW). Oracle announced a 50,000-unit AMD Instinct MI450 GPU deployment for the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster, with initial deployment in Q3 2026. MI450 features 432GB HBM4 memory, 20 TB/s bandwidth, 40 PFLOPs FP4 per GPU. Demand has exceeded AMD's own internal 2027 projections.

items.headline: AMD Advancing AI 2026 — July 22-23 at Moscone Center; Q2 results August 4
items.detail: AMD Advancing AI 2026 — July 22-23 at Moscone Center in San Francisco — is expected to formally launch MI450, preview EPYC Venice (Zen 6) CPUs, confirm Helios rack-scale systems, and provide a first look at the MI500 series targeting 2027. Analysts project the MI400 series will generate approximately $7.2 billion in revenue in its first year. Q2 2026 financial results: August 4.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Perplexity Personal Computer — an always-on AI running on a dedicated Mac mini — launched yesterday (June 30). Comet browser is now available for Enterprise with MDM deployment. CEO Aravind Srinivas reaffirmed a 2028 IPO target. ARR: $500M (+335% YoY); 100M+ MAU; $20B valuation. CoreWeave's 10-14% selloff today does not affect Perplexity's dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 inference clusters under its multi-year CoreWeave agreement.

items.headline: Personal Computer launched — always-on AI on dedicated Mac mini; Comet Enterprise available
items.detail: Perplexity announced Personal Computer — an always-on AI running on a dedicated Mac mini that merges local files, apps, and sessions with Perplexity Computer to monitor triggers, execute proactive tasks, and carry work forward around the clock. Voice Mode brings natural conversation to Personal Computer. Comet browser is now available for Enterprise organizations with MDM deployment across macOS and Windows. Comet defaults to Claude Opus 4.6 for complex reasoning tasks.

items.headline: 2028 IPO reaffirmed; $500M ARR; 100M MAU; $20B valuation; $750M Azure deal
items.detail: CEO Aravind Srinivas publicly reaffirmed a 2028 IPO target, stating Perplexity is not accelerating its timeline despite Anthropic and OpenAI moving toward public markets. Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are confirmed dead. ARR reached $500 million (+335% YoY) with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20 billion valuation. A $750M Azure deal provides cloud infrastructure. Perplexity Computer runs as native side panels in Microsoft 365 apps.

items.headline: CoreWeave selloff doesn't affect Perplexity's dedicated GB200 NVL72 inference contract
items.detail: CoreWeave's 10-14% stock drop today on Meta cloud competition fears does not affect Perplexity's multi-year strategic partnership providing dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72-powered clusters for Perplexity's inference workloads (announced March 4, 2026). NVIDIA Rubin-based systems begin rolling into the partnership in H2 2026. The contract includes W&B Weave integration for model experimentation to production workflows.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: CRWV fell 10-14% to approximately $87 today after Bloomberg reported Meta is building a competing cloud business. Evercore ISI reiterated an Outperform rating and $150 price target, noting the $21 billion Meta contract through December 2032 has take-or-pay protections that insulate near-term revenue. CoreWeave ARIA (AI Research and Iteration Agent for Weights & Biases) entered public preview yesterday. Revenue backlog: $99.4B (+50% QoQ). FY2026 revenue guidance: $12-13B.

items.headline: CRWV fell 10-14% to ~$87 on Meta cloud competition — Evercore ISI maintains Outperform/$150
items.detail: CoreWeave shares fell 10-14% to approximately $87 today following Bloomberg's report that Meta is building a cloud business to sell excess AI capacity. Evercore ISI reiterated an Outperform rating and $150 price target, arguing the selloff overreacts: the $21 billion Meta contract through December 2032 has take-or-pay characteristics, meaning Meta cannot simply redirect spend. The 37-analyst average Buy rating with a $143.41 price target remains intact.

items.headline: ARIA enters public preview — AI Research and Iteration Agent for Weights & Biases
items.detail: CoreWeave launched CoreWeave ARIA (AI Research & Iteration Agent) into public preview yesterday (June 30), built directly into Weights & Biases using W&B Weave. ARIA can work through thousands of experiment runs and tens of thousands of metrics in minutes, surfacing insights researchers might miss and recommending model improvements. W&B Weave's agent development capabilities also entered general availability.

items.headline: Backlog $99.4B (+50% QoQ); FY2026 guidance $12-13B; Fable 5 restored — full Anthropic traffic resumes
items.detail: CoreWeave reaffirmed FY2026 revenue guidance of $12-13 billion with an $18-19 billion exit run rate. Revenue backlog surged 50% sequentially to $99.4 billion. Nine of the top ten AI model providers are CoreWeave customers. 2026 capex floor: $31 billion. Fable 5 restoration today means full Anthropic Claude traffic can now flow to CoreWeave Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters, improving utilization after 19 days of suspension.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: No major July 1 announcements from Mistral. The Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPUs) missed its end-of-June opening target with no official announcement yet. A secondary Les Ulis (Essonne) 10 MW inference facility targets Q3 2026. Funding discussions continue at approximately €20B ($23.2B) valuation targeting a $3.5B raise. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral as an additional model option alongside Gemini for Siri/iOS integration.

items.headline: Paris data center still pending; Les Ulis Q3 2026; 200 MW European compute roadmap on track
items.detail: Mistral AI's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris — 44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems providing approximately 276 exaflops of FP4 compute, operated by Eclairion — has not officially opened as of July 1 after missing its end-of-June target. A secondary Les Ulis (Essonne) 10 MW inference facility is scheduled for Q3 2026. Combined with a $1.4B Sweden deal, Mistral targets 200 MW across Europe by end of 2027.

items.headline: Mistral Medium 3.5 and Vibe platform launched; Apple evaluating Mistral for Siri integration
items.detail: Mistral introduced Mistral Medium 3.5, remote coding agents in Vibe (the unified agent platform combining Le Chat consumer and enterprise assistant), and Work mode for complex tasks, with Airbus, BMW Group, and ASML as named industrial launch customers. Sherwood News reports Apple is evaluating Mistral as an additional model option alongside Gemini for future Siri/iOS integration, which could be a significant enterprise distribution catalyst.

items.headline: Funding at ~€20B ($23.2B) valuation; $3.5B raise target; Mistral Compute sovereign GPU cloud launched
items.detail: Mistral AI is in discussions to raise approximately €3 billion ($3.5 billion) at a potential valuation near €20 billion ($23.2 billion), per Bloomberg. CEO Arthur Mensch targets €1 billion in 2026 revenue, up from €200 million in 2025. Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems, launched at VivaTech 2026 (June 20) — is the flagship infrastructure offering.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: TSLA hit $420 today on Cybercab and Optimus excitement. Optimus production is confirmed to begin in July 2026 with a goal of 50,000 units by year-end; several thousand units are already operating in Tesla's own factories. A Tesla executive confirmed the Cybercab will be under $30,000 within six months. FSD v14.3 is deployed with 20% reduced inference latency. Grok 4.5 is running in private beta inside Tesla.

items.headline: TSLA hits $420 — AI Robotaxi and Optimus hype drives volatility
items.detail: Tesla stock hit $420 on July 1 driven by excitement around the Cybercab robotaxi and Optimus humanoid robot production ramp. The Cybercab active fleet spans approximately 59-100+ units across Austin, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. EPA filing (June 19) confirmed production Cybercab specs: 47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range (293-mile adjusted).

items.headline: Optimus production begins July 2026 — 50K unit goal by year-end; external B2B customers late 2026
items.detail: Tesla's Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robot production begins this month. Several thousand units are already working in Tesla's own factories. First external commercial customers — enterprise/B2B deployments — are targeted for late 2026. Tesla's $25 billion capex plan includes substantial manufacturing equipment for robot assembly lines. Tesla's Terafab initiative targets in-house semiconductor fabrication for the AI5 chip. Gigafactory Texas North Campus expansion targets 10 million robots per year long term.

items.headline: Tesla executive: Cybercab under $30K within 6 months; Grok 4.5 in private beta inside Tesla
items.detail: A Tesla executive stated the Cybercab will be sold to customers for under $30,000 within the next six months, placing volume deliveries before end of 2026. FSD v14.3 is deployed with 20% reduced inference latency. Grok 4.5 (1.5T parameter V9 model trained on Cursor coding data) is running in private beta inside Tesla, reflecting the SpaceX/xAI compute sharing arrangement.

ipoTracker.status: Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export controls LIFTED today — both models available globally again; Claude Sonnet 5 launched as new default; Series H $65B at $965B post-money; ARR $47B; October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, targeting an October 2026 Nasdaq IPO led by Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley with an expected raise of $60B+. Today (July 1) is a landmark day: the U.S. Department of Commerce lifted export controls on Fable 5 and Mythos 5, restoring full global access to Anthropic's flagship models. Claude Sonnet 5 simultaneously launched as the new default Free/Pro model at near-Opus performance. The $65B Series H closed at a $965B post-money valuation, led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital, with $15B from hyperscalers including $5B from Amazon, plus Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix. ARR: $47B.
ipoTracker.note: Fable 5 restoration today removes the biggest pre-IPO operational risk that emerged in June. Claude Sonnet 5 launch reinforces model cadence. October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target remains intact.

latestArticle.title: Redeploying Claude Fable 5

ipoTracker.status: GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna restricted to ~20 orgs; broader access expected mid-July; IPO leaning 2027; 42-state AG probe ongoing; ChatGPT market share 46.4% — first below 50%
ipoTracker.process: OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC on June 8, 2026, with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading. The company is leaning toward a 2027 listing — CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness, Stargate cash burn, and SpaceX IPO volatility. GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted organizations as of July 1; broader access is expected mid-July. A coalition of 42 state AGs opened a formal probe June 12. ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share fell to 46.4% — first time below 50%, with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.
ipoTracker.note: GPT-5.6 government access restrictions and the 42-state AG probe are the dominant pre-IPO risk factors. GPT-5.6 broader access expected mid-July is the next positive catalyst. 2027 IPO posture remains the working assumption.

latestArticle.title: Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol: a next-generation model

ipoTracker.status: SPCX ~$153 (-32% from ATH $225.64 on June 16); Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 starts today; Grok 4.5 private beta at Tesla/SpaceX; $60B Cursor acquisition Q3 close on track; CSAM regulatory risk unresolved
ipoTracker.process: SpaceX priced at $135/share on June 11, raising $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation in the largest IPO ever recorded. Shares peaked at $225.64 on June 16 following the Cursor announcement. Reflection AI's $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts today (July 1). Grok 4.5 (1.5 trillion parameters, V9 model, Cursor training data) in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX. A 2T parameter model is in final training stages expected to complete late July. The $60B Cursor acquisition is on track for Q3 2026 close.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 12, 2026
ipoTracker.note: Reflection AI payments begin today. CSAM disclosure remains an unresolved regulatory risk. Cursor Q3 close is the next major corporate milestone.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX's $60 Billion Cursor Acquisition Changes Everything

ipoTracker.status: CBRS recovering after post-earnings selloff; Q1 2026 revenue $193.4M; $20B+ OpenAI compute deal (750MW); GPT-5.6 Sol Cerebras deployment planned this month
ipoTracker.process: Cerebras completed its Nasdaq IPO in May 2026 under ticker CBRS. Q1 2026 results (released June 23) massively beat estimates: $193.4M revenue vs. $56.65M expected (+92-94% YoY). Major deals: multi-year OpenAI compute deal (750MW, $20B+ valued, option to $30B) and a multi-year AWS partnership pairing Cerebras CS-3 with Trainium 3. OpenAI plans to launch GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month at up to 750 tokens/second.
ipoTracker.note: GPT-5.6 Sol Cerebras deployment this month is the next major positive catalyst. Post-earnings margin guidance selloff is the near-term stock risk.

latestArticle.title: Cerebras Systems Announces Strong First Quarter 2026 Results

ipoTracker.status: CRWV ~$87 today (-10-14% on Meta cloud news); Russell 3000 effective June 26; ARIA in public preview; backlog $99.4B (+50% QoQ); FY2026 guidance $12-13B; Evercore ISI Outperform/$150
ipoTracker.process: CoreWeave completed its Nasdaq IPO in March 2025 (priced at $40/share) and trades as CRWV. Russell 3000 inclusion became effective after June 26 close. CRWV fell 10-14% today to approximately $87 on Bloomberg's Meta Compute cloud report. Evercore ISI reiterated Outperform/$150 target, citing the $21B Meta take-or-pay contract through 2032. ARIA entered public preview yesterday. Revenue backlog: $99.4 billion (+50% QoQ). FY2026 revenue guidance: $12-13 billion. Fable 5 restoration today means full Anthropic Claude traffic can now flow to CoreWeave infrastructure.
ipoTracker.note: Meta cloud announcement is the dominant negative development today. Take-or-pay protects near-term revenue. CRWV at ~$87 vs $143 analyst average target represents a significant discount.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave Stock Drops Sharply on Report of Meta AI Cloud Business

ipoTracker.status: IPO confirmed June 9 at $42/share; raised $2.1B; $15B market cap; ticker HFCE on Nasdaq
ipoTracker.process: Hugging Face completed its Nasdaq IPO on June 9, 2026, pricing at $42 per share and raising $2.1 billion at a $15 billion market cap under ticker HFCE. The IPO is widely seen as an open-source AI watershed — the first major public listing of a company whose primary assets are open-source model repositories, training infrastructure, and developer community.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 9, 2026
ipoTracker.note: HFCE is a publicly traded open-source AI infrastructure bellwether. Track as an indicator of investor appetite for open-source AI business models.

latestArticle.title: Inside the Hugging Face IPO: What It Means for Open-Source AI in 2026

ipoTracker.stage: Watchlist candidate — CEO shifting to IPO-bound stance; H2 2026 or 2027 S-1 expected
ipoTracker.status: No filing; CEO acknowledges IPO path; $5.4B ARR at 65% YoY growth; $165-175B valuation target
ipoTracker.process: Databricks CEO Ali Ghodsi has shifted to an explicitly IPO-bound posture targeting 2027 or H2 2026 at $165-175 billion valuation. The company has $5.4 billion ARR growing 65% YoY with positive free cash flow. SpaceXAI announced native Grok availability on Databricks Agent Bricks at the 2026 Data + AI Summit (June 18). No filing confirmed.
ipoTracker.confidence: H2 2026 or 2027 S-1 expected per reporting; no filing confirmed
ipoTracker.note: CEO's tone has shifted toward IPO-bound. Strong fundamentals support a $165-175B valuation. Watch for S-1 filing announcement.

latestArticle.title: IPO-bound Databricks reportedly eyes $175B valuation after hitting $5.4B revenue run rate

ipoTracker.status: Unverified filing claim; no primary-source confirmation; $14B+ raised June 2025
ipoTracker.process: Some IPO tracker sites continue to claim Scale AI filed an S-1 in March 2026, but no matching SEC filing, Scale AI announcement, or credible outlet confirmation has been found as of July 1. Scale raised more than $14 billion in June 2025 and is unlikely to pursue a near-term IPO given its recent large private financing.
ipoTracker.confidence: Low until verified by primary source
ipoTracker.note: No change from prior checks. No SEC EDGAR record for Scale AI found. Tracker-only claims remain unconfirmed.

latestArticle.title: Can You Invest in Scale AI Pre-IPO?

ipoTracker.stage: High-growth private company — 2028 IPO target confirmed; Apple acquisition talks dead
ipoTracker.status: Personal Computer launched June 30; Comet Enterprise available; CEO reaffirmed 2028 IPO target; $500M ARR; 100M MAU; $20B valuation; CoreWeave GB200 NVL72 inference partnership
ipoTracker.process: Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are dead. CEO Aravind Srinivas publicly stated a preference for an independent 2028 IPO. ARR reached $500 million (+335% YoY) with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20 billion valuation. Perplexity Personal Computer launched June 30. Comet Enterprise is now available with MDM deployment. CoreWeave's multi-year inference partnership uses dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters. CoreWeave's selloff today does not affect the dedicated inference contract.
ipoTracker.confidence: No filing; Apple acquisition confirmed dead; 2028 IPO is stated preference
ipoTracker.note: Personal Computer launch extends product surface into ambient/proactive AI. 2028 IPO timeline is firm.

latestArticle.title: Perplexity Personal Computer — Release Notes June 2026

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; Colossus 1 serving Anthropic at ~$1.25B/month; Colossus 2 now serving Reflection AI at $150M/month as of today; Fable 5 restored — full Claude traffic resumes
partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceXAI provides Anthropic access to Colossus 1 at approximately $1.25 billion per month through May 2029. Colossus 2 began serving Reflection AI today (July 1) at $150M/month, with both Colossus facilities now committed to third parties. Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export controls were lifted today, restoring full global Claude access and enabling resumed normal workload flow to Colossus 1.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed relationship; monthly payment figure from reporting, not company-confirmed
partnershipTracker.note: Fable 5 restored today is a direct positive for Colossus 1 utilization. Reflection AI $150M/month starts today on Colossus 2. Both facilities fully committed.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX signs $6.3 billion compute deal with Reflection AI for Colossus data center

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; $100B+/10-year, 5GW Trainium; Project Rainier active at ~500K chips; Fable 5 restored unlocks full Claude on Bedrock; AWS Summit DC concluded today
partnershipTracker.terms: The April 2026 expanded agreement commits more than $100 billion over 10 years, securing up to 5GW of Trainium2/3/4 capacity. Project Rainier runs nearly half a million Trainium2 chips for Claude workloads, with 1M+ chips expected by year end. Amazon contributed $5B as a hyperscaler investor in the $65B Series H. Fable 5 restoration today unlocks full Claude model access on Bedrock for the first time since June 12. AWS Summit DC's ICAMF and ASCI announcements expand the federal market for Claude on Bedrock.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by company and partner
partnershipTracker.note: Fable 5 restoration today is the most significant positive development for this relationship since June 12. Full Claude access on Bedrock is restored.

latestArticle.title: AWS Summit DC 2026: Billions in AI and cloud investment for public sector

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; ~3.5GW TPU capacity starting 2027; Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform operational; AVGO down 16.5% past month — does not affect TPU supply terms
partnershipTracker.terms: Anthropic's agreement with Google and Broadcom secures approximately 3.5 gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity expected to start coming online in 2027. Google has invested up to $40 billion in Anthropic. The Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform (operational since June 9) facilitates Anthropic's 1+ gigawatt compute expansion at Fluidstack-based sites beginning mid-2026 using Broadcom XPUs. Gemini 3.5 Pro's continued delay does not affect the TPU supply relationship.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by company
partnershipTracker.note: AVGO stock decline is market-driven, not a partnership development. TPU capacity start 2027. No structural change to this relationship today.

latestArticle.title: Apollo leads $35 billion capital solution for Broadcom AI XPV Platform

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, model distribution, investment, GPU optimization
partnershipTracker.status: Milestone today: Claude models in Microsoft Foundry on GB300 Blackwell Ultra now generally available; Copilot Cowork billing active; M365 pricing increases today
partnershipTracker.terms: Anthropic has committed to purchase $30 billion of Azure compute capacity. Claude models in Microsoft Foundry — hosted on Azure and running on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs — are now generally available as of today. Microsoft and Anthropic remain in active negotiations to deploy Claude on Maia 200 custom AI accelerators for inference. Copilot Cowork billing activated today (July 1).
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed; Claude in Foundry GA milestone achieved today
partnershipTracker.note: Claude in Microsoft Foundry GA today on GB300 Blackwell Ultra is the key milestone for this relationship. Maia 200 negotiations remain the significant pending item.

partnershipTracker.terms: Microsoft remains OpenAI's primary cloud partner under a non-exclusive IP license through 2032 (amended April 27, 2026). GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted organizations; broader access expected mid-July. Copilot Cowork billing activated today (July 1). Microsoft 365 commercial pricing increases took effect today.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by company
partnershipTracker.note: Copilot Cowork billing now active. GPT-5.6 government access restrictions continue to limit the top-tier model availability on Foundry. Broader access mid-July is the next milestone.

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, model distribution, and strategic infrastructure partnership
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; GPT-5.5 and Codex on Bedrock GA; GPT-5.6 restricted; AWS Summit DC federal expansion broadens Bedrock market
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI's frontier models (GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4) and Codex are generally available on AWS Bedrock since June 1. GPT-5.6 remains restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted organizations. Amazon invested $50 billion in the March 2026 Stargate funding round. AWS Summit DC ICAMF and ASCI expand the federal market for AI workloads on Bedrock. OpenAI holds a 2GW Trainium capacity contract.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by company
partnershipTracker.note: AWS Summit DC federal announcements expand the government AI market relevant to OpenAI on Bedrock. GPT-5.6 mid-July broader release is the next key Bedrock milestone.

partnershipTracker.type: GPU infrastructure and strategic supplier relationship
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; 10GW partnership; H2 2026 first-gigawatt Vera Rubin deployment on track
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA agreed to deploy at least 10GW of NVIDIA systems for OpenAI infrastructure, with the first gigawatt targeted for H2 2026 on Vera Rubin. NVIDIA invested $30 billion in Stargate in March 2026. NVIDIA runs 81% of TOP500 supercomputers; FY2026 revenue $215.9B (+65% YoY). The Palantir-NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System today demonstrates NVIDIA's expanding government AI footprint aligned with OpenAI's federal market ambitions.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by company
partnershipTracker.note: H2 2026 first-gigawatt Vera Rubin deployment remains the next major milestone. No change to the relationship today.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and NVIDIA Announce Strategic Partnership to Deploy 10 Gigawatts of NVIDIA Systems

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI; 1GW H2 2026 rollout; Oracle 50K MI450 supercluster validates demand; AMD Advancing AI July 22-23
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI and AMD agreed on a multi-generation 6GW AMD GPU deployment beginning with an initial 1GW MI450 rollout in H2 2026. AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to OpenAI. Oracle's 50,000-unit MI450 deployment for a public AI supercluster (Q3 2026) validates AMD inference demand. MI450 demand has exceeded AMD's own internal 2027 projections. AMD Advancing AI 2026 (July 22-23) is expected to formally disclose MI450 production details.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by company; samples shipping per AMD CEO
partnershipTracker.note: AMD Advancing AI July 22-23 is the next milestone for deployment disclosures. Q2 financial results August 4.

latestArticle.title: AMD and OpenAI announce strategic partnership to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD GPUs

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed platform; CRWV fell 10-14% today on Meta cloud news; Jalapeño H2 2026 Stargate deployment target; Abilene TX flagship fully operational
partnershipTracker.terms: Stargate spans Oracle (up to 4.5GW, $300B+ over five years), SoftBank (two sites scaling to 1.5GW), and CoreWeave (up to $22.4B across three expansions). Amazon invested $50B and NVIDIA invested $30B in March 2026. Abilene TX flagship (1.2GW on Oracle Cloud) is fully operational. Jalapeño (OpenAI-Broadcom custom chip) targets H2 2026 Stargate deployment. CoreWeave's 10-14% stock decline today does not change the contracted Stargate backlog commitments.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed platform; evolving structure
partnershipTracker.note: CRWV selloff today is a stock event, not a contract event. Jalapeño H2 2026 deployment remains on track.

latestArticle.title: Building the compute infrastructure for the Intelligence Age

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; $20B+ for 750MW; GPT-5.6 Sol Cerebras deployment planned this month at 750 tok/sec
partnershipTracker.terms: Cerebras announced a multi-year compute deal with OpenAI for 750 megawatts of inference capacity valued at more than $20 billion, with option to scale to $30 billion. OpenAI confirmed Codex-Spark on Cerebras achieves more than 1,000 tokens per second for coding tasks. OpenAI plans to deploy GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month at up to 750 tokens per second for select customers.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by Cerebras alongside Q1 2026 earnings
partnershipTracker.note: GPT-5.6 Sol Cerebras deployment this month is the key near-term milestone. At $20B+, this is on par with CoreWeave's Meta deal ($21B).

latestArticle.title: Cerebras Systems Announces Strong First Quarter 2026 Results

partnershipTracker.type: Custom AI silicon partnership — Jalapeño LLM inference accelerator
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; Jalapeño unveiled June 24; H2 2026 Stargate deployment target; AVGO down 16.5% past month — does not affect partnership
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled Jalapeño on June 24 — OpenAI's first custom AI accelerator, a reticle-sized ASIC designed for LLM inference. Co-developed from design to tape-out in nine months. Initial deployment targets H2 2026 in Stargate data centers. OpenAI is Broadcom's sixth confirmed custom-silicon customer. AVGO is down 16.5% over the past month as AI trade cools — does not change the Jalapeño program fundamentals.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies
partnershipTracker.note: H2 2026 Stargate deployment is the next milestone. AVGO stock decline is market-driven, not a partnership issue.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and Broadcom reveal Jalapeño, first AI chip in partnership

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed $21B through December 2032; CRWV fell 10-14% today on Meta cloud competition news; take-or-pay structure protects near-term revenue
partnershipTracker.terms: CoreWeave provides Meta AI cloud capacity through December 2032 under a roughly $21 billion agreement, including NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 deployments. Meta's announcement today that it is building its own competing cloud business (Meta Compute) caused CRWV to fall 10-14%. Evercore ISI notes the $21B contract has take-or-pay protections — Meta cannot simply redirect spend. This contract is among the anchor commitments in CoreWeave's $99.4 billion backlog.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by CoreWeave
partnershipTracker.note: Meta Compute announcement today creates a structural tension: Meta is both CoreWeave's largest customer and a potential future competitor. Take-or-pay protects revenue through 2032 but post-contract renewal is now uncertain.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave and Meta Announce $21 Billion Expanded AI Infrastructure Agreement

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed multi-year agreement; Fable 5 restored today — full Claude traffic can now flow to CoreWeave; CRWV fell 10-14% on Meta cloud news but Anthropic contract is unaffected
partnershipTracker.terms: CoreWeave will run Claude workloads at production scale for Anthropic under a multi-year agreement (April 10, 2026), with infrastructure rolling out in phases beginning later in 2026 on Vera Rubin NVL72 systems. Financial terms remain undisclosed. Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export controls were lifted today, restoring full global Claude access and enabling normal inference workload flow to CoreWeave infrastructure for the first time since June 12.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by CoreWeave
partnershipTracker.note: Fable 5 restoration today is the most important positive development for this relationship since June 12. Utilization can now recover to normal levels.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave Announces Multi-Year Agreement With Anthropic

partnershipTracker.status: Fully restored today — Fable 5 and Mythos 5 both available globally; Palantir PLTR up 9% on NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS; Maven Smart System retains DoD designation
partnershipTracker.terms: Claude runs inside Palantir's Maven Smart System on AWS infrastructure for U.S. government intelligence and defense users. Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export controls were lifted today (July 1), restoring full Claude model access for government deployments. Palantir's NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System — running Nemotron models on air-gapped Blackwell Ultra — is a complementary but distinct product from this Claude partnership. Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed partnership; now fully restored with both Fable 5 and Mythos 5
partnershipTracker.note: Fable 5 restoration today returns this pipeline to full capacity. Palantir-NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS serves air-gapped environments with open models — complementary to, not competing with, the Claude-on-Palantir-on-AWS architecture.

latestArticle.title: Redeploying Claude Fable 5

partnershipTracker.type: Sovereign AI Operating System — government and defense deployment
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; announced June 29; PLTR up 9% today to new all-time valuation high; Nemotron open models on air-gapped Blackwell Ultra for U.S. agencies
partnershipTracker.terms: Palantir and NVIDIA announced the Sovereign AI Operating System on June 29 — a combined hardware and software platform enabling U.S. government agencies and critical infrastructure operators to deploy NVIDIA Nemotron open models in air-gapped Blackwell Ultra environments. Agencies own the model weights and training data on their own hardware and can customize Nemotron models without public network exposure. The partnership drove Palantir stock up 9% to $127.31 today — a new all-time valuation high.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies
partnershipTracker.note: New confirmed partnership as of June 29. The Sovereign AI OS uses NVIDIA Nemotron open models — distinct from the Anthropic-Palantir-AWS relationship which uses Claude. The two complement each other by serving different customer security and model-ownership requirements.

latestArticle.title: Palantir Launches Engine for Deploying NVIDIA Nemotron Open Models in Sovereign Environments

partnershipTracker.status: Strained — Gemini deal sidelined OpenAI placement; OpenAI retained outside counsel; breach-of-contract notice still pending; no lawsuit filed
partnershipTracker.terms: Apple and OpenAI maintain an existing integration bringing ChatGPT into Siri and Apple Intelligence. Apple's Google Gemini deal (WWDC June 8) displaced OpenAI as the primary Siri AI engine. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini in Siri) is disabled pending EU DMA clarity. OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice alleging Apple made 'no honest effort' to meet integration commitments. No lawsuit has been filed as of July 1. Apple is also evaluating Mistral as an additional model option.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed integration; breach-of-contract claim reported but not confirmed
partnershipTracker.note: OpenAI's formal breach-of-contract notice remains the key pending legal development. Apple evaluating Mistral adds further competitive pressure on OpenAI placement.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI considering legal action against Apple over Siri integration

partnershipTracker.terms: Apple struck a reported $1 billion per year deal for Google Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri AI, announced at WWDC 2026 (June 8). Siri AI runs on Google Cloud infrastructure; GA September 2026. EU launch delayed pending DMA regulatory clarity. Gemini 3.5 Pro remains delayed as of July 1 with no confirmed date — maintaining delivery risk ahead of Apple's September iPhone launch window.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Reported by credible outlets; not officially confirmed by Apple or Google
partnershipTracker.note: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains delayed with no confirmed July date as of today. This is the highest-priority watch item for the Apple-Google relationship heading into Q3.

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; Paris data center still pending; Mistral Compute sovereign GPU cloud with 18K Grace Blackwell systems operational; Apple evaluating Mistral
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (June 20). Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition. Mistral's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris has not officially opened as of July 1 after missing its end-of-June target. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral for Siri/iOS integration.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies at VivaTech
partnershipTracker.note: Paris data center opening now expected in July. Apple's Mistral evaluation is a potential major new distribution relationship.

latestArticle.title: France Advances Europe's AI Future With NVIDIA Technologies

partnershipTracker.type: AI compute contract — Colossus 2 data center access
partnershipTracker.status: Active as of today (July 1) — $150M/month payments begin; Colossus 2 dedicated to Reflection AI through 2029
partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceX's $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI (valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI) is now active as of today (July 1) at $150 million per month for NVIDIA GB300 GPU access via the Colossus 2 data center in Memphis through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months. Both Colossus 1 (Anthropic) and Colossus 2 (Reflection AI) are now fully committed.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by CNBC reporting; payments begin today
partnershipTracker.note: July 1 start date is today — payments begin. With both Colossus facilities committed to third parties, xAI's own future dedicated compute access for Grok model training remains an open question.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX AI Colossus data center signs deal with Reflection AI

partnershipTracker.type: Structured finance platform for Broadcom AI XPU deployment
partnershipTracker.status: Operational — $35B capital solution; enables 20GW+ Broadcom XPU compute; AVGO down 16.5% past month; six custom silicon customers confirmed
partnershipTracker.terms: Apollo and Blackstone are leading a $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform, enabling more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute capacity using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. The platform is operational since June 9. Jalapeño (the OpenAI-Broadcom custom chip unveiled June 24) confirms OpenAI as Broadcom's sixth custom-silicon customer. AVGO is down approximately 16.5% over the past month — does not change the platform terms.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by Apollo press release and Broadcom announcement
partnershipTracker.note: AVGO stock decline is market-driven, not a partnership issue. Six custom-silicon customers now confirmed. FY2027 $100B+ AI revenue target is the long-term anchor.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and Broadcom reveal Jalapeño, first AI chip in partnership
